    echart4r Bar Chart

    echart_bar_chart.Rd

    echart4r Bar Chart

    Usage

    echart_bar_chart(
  t,
  x,
  y,
  est,
  fill,
  moe = NULL,
  pos = NULL,
  column_vs_bar = "column",
  ...
)

    Arguments

    t

    A data frame in long form for plotting

    x

    The name of the category variable

    y

    The name of the variable with numeric values to plot

    est

    Select "number", "percent", or "currency" - defaults to "percent"

    fill

    The name of the variable you want the fill color of the bars or lines to be based on

    moe

    The name of the margin of error variable

    pos

    Determines if the bars are side-by-side (NULL) or stacked ("grp") - defaults to NULL

    column_vs_bar

    Select "column" or "bar" for chart orientation - defaults to "column"

    ...

    additional arguments passed to generic_echart

    Value

    An interactive bar or column chart via echarts4r

    Author

    Christy Lam

    Examples

    library(tidyverse)
#> ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
#> ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ readr     2.1.5
#> ✔ ggplot2   3.5.1     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
#> ✔ lubridate 1.9.3     ✔ tibble    3.2.1
#> ✔ purrr     1.0.2     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
#> ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
#> ✖ readr::edition_get()   masks testthat::edition_get()
#> ✖ dplyr::filter()        masks psrcplot::filter(), stats::filter()
#> ✖ purrr::is_null()       masks testthat::is_null()
#> ✖ dplyr::lag()           masks stats::lag()
#> ✖ readr::local_edition() masks testthat::local_edition()
#> ✖ tidyr::matches()       masks dplyr::matches(), testthat::matches()
#> ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors

d <- mode_share_example_data |>
 filter(Year == 2020 & Category == 'Population by Race') |>
 mutate(Race = str_wrap(Race, 20))

color_palette <- c('#8dd3c7','#ffffb3','#bebada','#fb8072','#80b1d3')

echart_bar_chart(t = d,
                x = "Race",
                y = "share",
                fill = "Geography",
                color = color_palette,
                title = "Population by Race",
                subtitle = "In 2020",
                pos = NULL,
                column_vs_bar = "bar",
                est = "number")
